content.stem: Which number follows a different rule than the others: 6, 9, 12, 17
content.explanation: The numbers 6, 9, and 12 are all multiples of 3 (6=3×2, 9=3×3, 12=3×4). The number 17 is not a multiple of 3, so it doesn't belong.

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Students may think the sequence increases by 3 each time and identify 6 as breaking the pattern from an assumed starting point
Students may incorrectly assume the sequence should be 6, 9, 12, 15 and identify 9 as problematic when comparing to their expected arithmetic progression
Students may focus on 12 as the largest number that fits their assumed arithmetic sequence and think it doesn't belong because it ends the pattern
